name: Session 3 preparation
summary:
**Conversation Overview**

Jake is the designer and player of a custom soulslike tabletop RPG called "The Deep Salts," which he maintains in a GitHub repository (pixel549/the-deep-salts). Claude serves as the Dungeon Master, accessing the repo at the start of each session to read campaign state, character data, and session planning documents. The game features a Bloodborne-inspired aesthetic with real-time stopwatch mechanics for parrying, called shots, and weapon detonations, set in a gothic horror world centered on a decaying hotel called Stillwell Hydro. Jake is both the system's designer and its player, meaning he actively spots and corrects mechanical inconsistencies mid-session, expects concrete numbers on the spot rather than deferred rulings, and treats live play as a design validation process.

Session 3 covered hub activity (shopping with vendor Maud, discovering a new NPC engineer in the boiler wing who offered to build a ranged capsule launcher for 20 Salts), the first activation of a new biome called the Choir Deep, a successful stealth ambush kill of a Chapel Penitent via a headshot that accidentally cleared the sever threshold, and a losing encounter with a new boss called The Singer — a rooted, non-melee entity that applies Influence stacks passively each round. Lloyd escaped via Abandon-All, forfeiting all carried gear (knuckledusters, Beckoner idol, Charm, Wooden Token) to a future Singer instance, leaving him weaponless. Session 3 prompted a major rules overhaul: turn order was replaced (player always acts first, eliminating the d20 roll-off), Abandon-All and Retreat were split into two distinct escape mechanics with different costs and combat restrictions, ambush called shots on fully unaware targets were ruled Tier-1-parry-equivalent and confirmed to stack multiplicatively with the visceral bonus, Insight window bonuses were clarified as flat-per-tier rather than cumulative, Powder Charge detonation timing was canonized at 1.00s ±0.15s, and the Insanity tick threshold from failed Influence saves was confirmed at stack 3+. A Singer boss Monster Manual entry was written. Jake also requested and received two workflow improvements: a "sessions since" rotation tracker added to the ephemeral DM plan document to prevent narrative threads from stagnating, and a reformatted DM plan structure focused on last-session outcomes versus this-session intentions.

Jake's stated preferences and corrections throughout: he wants DM errors flagged openly rather than silently patched; he expects all mechanical rulings to produce concrete numbers immediately; he caught and corrected several live DM errors (incorrect multiplier stacking on the ambush kill, offering a "blind parry" that doesn't exist in the system, handing back a turn that had already been consumed by a compulsion effect, under-running enemy approach movement for several rounds); he prefers real stopwatch checks over in-chat timing widgets; and he wants SVG battle maps generated when combat has enough bodies to require spatial tracking. All session-end documentation — ruleset, design bible, dev log, character sheet, campaign log, and DM plan — was updated and pushed to GitHub before closing.

**Tool Knowledge**

Claude accesses the GitHub repo by constructing the clone URL inline with the PAT provided by Jake in-chat, using a `sed` pipe to redact the token from any terminal output: `git clone "https://[PAT]@github.com/pixel549/the-deep-salts.git" . 2>&1 | sed 's/github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]*/[REDACTED]/g'`. Git commits use inline author config (`git -c user.email="dm@deepsalts.local" -c user.name="Deep Salts DM"`) since no global git config exists in the environment. The PAT is never stored; Jake provides it fresh each session. File edits use `str_replace` for targeted changes to existing documents and `create_file` or heredoc bash writes for new files or full rewrites. SVG battle maps are written via `create_file` to `/home/claude/`, then copied to `/mnt/user-data/outputs/` and surfaced with `present_files`. The repo contains five canonical documents: `deep-salts-v7.1.md` (ruleset), `deep-salts-design-bible-v2.6.md` (monster manual and DM guidance), `deep-salts-dev-log

content.text:
1	# THE DEEP SALTS — Campaign Log
     2	*Narrative continuity only. For mechanical state see `deep-salts-character-sheet.md`. For rules/design history see the ruleset, design bible, and dev log. If you're a fresh instance picking this up cold, read this file first, then the character sheet.*
     3	
     4	---
     5	
     6	## Where things stand
     7	
     8	**Prettyboy Lloyd** just completed his second real run and is back at the hub (Stillwell Hydro), Vault banked, HP and capsules restored, Insight risen to 6 (Tier 3 — the Charm is now usable for the first time). He entered the same salt recipe as his first run (rerolled layout), fought past a rooted, leashed floor-creature in a flooded gallery without killing it, looted a chapel room (a sentient-seeming idol later identified as "The Beckoner," a keepsake token, a burned journal), met a recurring old man NPC who appears to persist across rerolls of this recipe specifically, and found this run's anchor before backtracking to explore further and banking clean. The Choir Deep recipe is still unbrewed, still sitting in his room. Next session should pick up at the hub — shopping/prepping, trying the now-usable Charm, deciding on the Beckoner's Influence-cost tradeoff, or finally brewing the Choir Deep to descend somewhere new.
     9	
    10	## Quests (persistent — track these)
    11	
    12	*Per the Instance Quest ruling (Ruleset §18, session 2): only threads anchored to persistent entities — recurring NPCs, the hub, recipes, carried items — survive rerolls. Everything else died with its layout and is deliberately not tracked.*
    13	
    14	- **The old man by the fire.** The one known reroll-persistent NPC. First met partially mutated behind an Insight-gated door (session zero/one); met again this session in a completely different layout of the same recipe — "whatever I am now, I'm not one of the things that gets reshuffled with the room." Claims to have run this world's version of recipe-runs countless times until one run he touched the clean thing and "didn't end up back at any Hydro." No-names rule when speaking with him, unexplained. Talkative, trades honest question for honest question, currently the best lead on how the world works. **Open request:** bring him a taste of genuinely clean source-water if one is found (explicitly optional, his words). **Open question:** whether he persists in *other* recipes too — he doesn't know himself; brewing the Choir Deep will test it.
    15	- **The reception clerk's "brother" comments.** Referenced multiple times across sessions; Lloyd has avoided confirming or denying having a brother. Clerk is the hub's designated recurring-antagonist figure. Deliberate long-burn hook, still open.
    16	- **The Choir Deep.** Recipe identified, unbrewed, sitting in Lloyd's room two sessions running. Doubles as the persistence experiment for the old man.
    17	- **The Beckoner (carried item).** Instance quest *completed* — encountered, recovered, identified in-run, extracted as a persistent item. Its ongoing tradeoff (1 Influence stack on Lloyd per use, uncleared while carried) is live whenever he first uses it. Its warning lore ("if it asks for your name, don't—") travels with it.
    18	- **The Charm (carried item).** Now usable for the first time — Insight sits exactly at its 6+ gate. Untested.
    19	- **The Wooden Token (carried item).** Keepsake of "R.," a person from a now-expired instance. No mechanical effect; kept for whatever narrative weight it accrues. The R. story itself is gone with its layout — the token is all that escaped.
    20	
    21	## Expired instance quests (session 2 — not tracked, listed once for flavor continuity only)
    22	
    23	The dragger (fought, never killed — any future dragger is a fresh instance at full pool), "R." and the cell, the burned journal's author, the chapel and its offerings, the scratched door, both unexplored forks. All lost to the reroll, as intended.
    24	
    25	## Established tone / running notes for continuity
    26	
    27	- Combat resolves fast and concrete — numbers first, minimal narrative padding on routine hits. Description gets more room for kills, called shots, and narrative beats (NPC dialogue, environment, discoveries).
    28	- Player prefers real stopwatch checks (their own phone/count) over blind in-chat timing widgets where possible — the blind widget attempt in session zero was a genuine miss on DM's part, not a mechanic to repeat.
    29	- Session-zero rulings (visceral charges removed, Influence uncapped, enemy limb stagger duration, etc.) are now written directly into ruleset v7.1 and design bible v2.6 — a fresh instance reading those documents will already have them; no need to re-explain or re-litigate.
    30	- Jake (the player) is also this game's designer — expect him to spot rule inconsistencies mid-play and to want them resolved with concrete numbers on the spot, not deferred. Flag gaps rather than silently patching them, same as the design-process norms already established.
    31	- Session 2 introduced live DM-improvised mechanics mid-fight (a "Dragged" status, Heavy-attacks-break-grapples ruling) that got defined properly on request rather than left vague — same pattern going forward: improvise when needed, then nail down the number the moment it's asked for.
    32	- Session 2 caught two DM math errors (stale HP after a hub heal; a missed Rally recovery) — both corrected in play when Jake flagged them. Worth double-checking HP/Rally math proactively rather than waiting to be caught.
    33	
    34	---
    35	*Last updated: end of session 2.*
    36

The dragger (fought, never killed — any future dragger is a fresh instance at full pool), "R." and the cell, the burned journal's author, the chapel and its offerings, the scratched door, both unexplored forks. All lost to the reroll, as intended.

## Established tone / running notes for continuity

- Combat resolves fast and concrete — numbers first, minimal narrative padding on routine hits. Description gets more room for kills, called shots, and narrative beats (NPC dialogue, environment, discoveries).
- Player prefers real stopwatch checks (their own phone/count) over blind in-chat timing widgets where possible — the blind widget attempt in session zero was a genuine miss on DM's part, not a mechanic to repeat.
- Session-zero rulings (visceral charges removed, Influence uncapped, enemy limb stagger duration, etc.) are now written directly into ruleset v7.1 and design bible v2.6 — a fresh instance reading those documents will already have them; no need to re-explain or re-litigate.
- Jake (the player) is also this game's designer — expect him to spot rule inconsistencies mid-play and to want them resolved with concrete numbers on the spot, not deferred. Flag gaps rather than silently patching them, same as the design-process norms already established.

- Session 2 caught two DM math errors (stale HP after a hub heal; a missed Rally recovery) — both corrected in play when Jake flagged them. Worth double-checking HP/Rally math proactively rather than waiting to be caught.

---
*Last updated: end of session 2.*

1	# THE DEEP SALTS — Character Sheet (Live State)
     2	*DM-maintained. Rewritten after any significant change. Single source of truth for HP, tracks, charges, limbs, Insight, and inventory mid-fight.*
     3	
     4	---
     5	
     6	## "Prettyboy" Lloyd
     7	
     8	**Concept:** Young, athletic fighter. Blonde, blue-eyed, weary. Self-made twin knuckledusters rigged with detonating powder capsules.
     9	
    10	**Level:** 2 · **Insight:** 6 *(Tier 3 — up from 4: first sighting of a new archetype + reading substantive lore, session 2)*
    11	
    12	### Attributes
    13	| VIG | END | STR | SKI | RES |
    14	|---|---|---|---|---|
    15	| 8 | 10 | 4 | 17 | 3 |
    16	
    17	### Derived Stats
    18	- **Max HP:** 108 (current: 108 — full restoration at hub)
    19	- **Movement budget:** 8m *(base raised 5→8, session 2 movement rework — formula now 8 + ⌊End÷15⌋)*
    20	- **Attribute Modifier (generic uncertain-action checks only, session 2):** Skill 17 → **+0** · Strength 4 → **−2** · Resolve 3 → **−2**
    21	- **Insanity/Influence save mod:** ⌊Resolve÷10⌋ = +0 (Resolve 3 — still below the 10 threshold, no change yet)
    22	- *(Visceral charges removed as a resource — house ruling, session zero: parries gated by timing + real opening only, not a charge pool)*
    23	- *(Standard enemies cannot parry/visceral Lloyd as of session 2 — that loop is boss/elite-tier only)*
    24	
    25	### Weapon — Metal Knuckles (paired)
    26	- Type: Quick · Stat/Grade: Skill / D · One-handed, paired
    27	- Base Damage: 22
    28	- Light = 22 + ESV×0.5 · Heavy = 44 + ESV×0.75
    29	- **Current (ESV 17):** Light 30 (costs 1m) · Heavy 57 (costs 3m, session 2 movement rework)
    30	- **Passive Gimmick — Powder Charge:** Load a capsule into a knuckle (Fast Action, 0m). Landing a Heavy with one loaded triggers a detonation stopwatch check:
    31	  - **Hit window:** +50% of that heavy's raw damage; target takes 1 Discombobulation (Tick).
    32	  - **Miss window:** capsule vents wrong; Lloyd takes 1 self-inflicted Blood Loss stack.
    33	  - Capsule consumed either way.
    34	- **Capsules carried:** 4/4 · restocked at hub (spent 3, all successful hits, session 2)
    35	
    36	### Status Tracks
    37	| Track | Value |
    38	|---|---|
    39	| Blood Loss | 0 |
    40	| Insanity | 0 |
    41	| Influence | 0 |
    42	| Discombobulation | — (expired) |
    43	| Corrosion | 0 |
    44	| Burning | — |
    45	| Fugue | N/A |
    46	
    47	### Limbs
    48	Standard, no mutations, no damage.
    49	
    50	### White Salts
    51	- Purse: 0
    52	- Vault: 15 *(9 + 6 banked from session 2 run)*
    53	
    54	### Inventory
    55	*(Durability tags per §8: Constant / Hub Kit / Uses: N / Instance-specific)*
    56	- Metal Knuckles ×2 (equipped) — **Constant**
    57	- Powder capsules ×4/4 — **Hub Kit** (resets to 4 on hub return)
    58	- Wrap ×2 (clears 2 Blood Loss each) — **Hub Kit**
    59	- Tonic ×2 (~10% max HP heal each, 11 HP) — **Hub Kit**
    60	- Camphor ×1 (clears 2 Insanity) — **Hub Kit**
    61	- Scour ×1 (clears 1 Corrosion, free action) — **Hub Kit**
    62	- Charm ×1 (Action Item — Insight-gated 6+, **now usable**; extra die or widened parry window) — **Uses: 1**, destroyed on use, not hub-refilled
    63	- **The Beckoner** (idol, Action Item, 2m) — **Constant**. Point at one creature in line of sight, speak an invitation: target takes 1 Influence stack. Cost per use: 1 Influence stack on Lloyd, sourced from the idol — doesn't clear while carried; only by dropping/Vaulting it.
    64	- Wooden Token (keepsake, no mechanical effect — belonged to "R.") — **Constant**
    65	- Source-water flask, clean variant (big heal, no Influence stack) — **Uses: 1**, gift, not hub-refilled
    66	
    67	### Known Recipes
    68	- The Choir Deep — identified via Maud, unexplored, still unbrewed, left in his room
    69	
    70	### Mutations
    71	None.
    72	
    73	---
    74	*Last updated: end of session 2 — full run banked (Purse 6 → Vault), Insight 4→6 (Charm now usable), capsules restocked to 4/4, HP restored to full. Idol identified as "The Beckoner" via NPC lore, not Maud/Insight. Gained a recurring NPC contact (the old man, location unconfirmed persistence across rerolls) and an open request from him (bring clean source-water). New gear: Beckoner idol, Wooden Token, clean Source-water flask.*
    75

content.text:
Pulled. Here's where we're at:

**Lloyd, back at Stillwell Hydro** — full HP, capsules restocked to 4/4, Insight 6 (Charm now usable for the first time), 15 White Salts in the Vault. Beckoner and Wooden Token in his kit, Choir Deep still unbrewed in his room.

Open threads on the table: shop/prep, test the Charm, decide whether to eat the Influence stack and use the Beckoner, or finally brew the Choir Deep and descend somewhere new. Old man's still waiting on that clean source-water if you want to follow through.

Also sitting in my back pocket from last session: the Skill-scaling flag and the rooted floor-creature needing a real stat block before any combat with it — I'll generate that block now if you're heading back to that same recipe, or hold it if you're brewing Choir Deep instead.

What's Lloyd doing first?
skill scaling and the rooted floor creature were sorted last lesson, no?

content.text:
Yeah, you're right — both got handled, my mistake for flagging them as still open.

- **Skill scaling:** fixed session 2. The generic uncertain-action check moved off flat `d20 + Attribute` onto a scaled Attribute Modifier table (0–1: −3, 2–4: −2, 5–9: −1, 10–19: +0, 20–39: +1, 40–79: +2, 80–99: +3). Your Skill 17 now nets **+0**, not an auto-win. Doesn't touch the Save Roll formula, which was already fine. Not retroactive.
- **Rooted floor-creature:** also resolved — you fought it in the flooded gallery and didn't kill it (left it rooted/leashed), so there's no stat block hanging open. If it comes back it's a fresh instance anyway per the Instance Quest rule.

Good catch. What's Lloyd doing first — shop, Charm, Beckoner, or brew the Choir Deep?

one more idea I have had - given you can now access GH to do this, I think you should sketch yourself a small session plan in a DM plan document that you read at the start of each new session (to remember what happened last session), then wipe and recreate to continue the story and events in the new session. It will add some consistency, and also allow you to plan for what rewards / challenges / etc you will throw at me across the session, so that they don't feel too aimless. It should not be big - just a few bullet points. all the big relevant data would go into the bigger documents; this one is just to give you a sense of direction. make sense?

content.thinking: I need to set up a new DM plan document that gets read at the start of each session and refreshed at the end. This should outline the session's direction—key beats, rewards, challenges, and other relevant details. I'll need to add this to the workflow instructions so future sessions know about it, create the file for this session, and update the memory accordingly. I need to draft the DM plan document for session 3, sketching out the key threads and beats I want to hit—the shop prep, the Charm test, the Beckoner influence decision, the Choir Deep option, and the lingering threads with the old man and clerk brother. I'll structure it as a brief, directional guide that gets wiped and rewritten after each session, then add this workflow to my memory instructions so future sessions know to read it at the start. I need to check the current memory edits list to see what's already tracked before adding this new workflow step.

input.description: Draft the ephemeral DM session plan for session 3
input.file_text:
# THE DEEP SALTS — DM Session Plan (ephemeral)
*Wiped and rewritten at the end of every session. This is direction and pacing only — no narrative or mechanical state lives here. For that, see the campaign log and character sheet. Read this at session start for a sense of where the session is headed; rewrite it fresh at session end for next time.*

---

## Session 3 — planned shape

- **Hub beats first, keep them light:** Charm test (low stakes, just resolve it), Beckoner Influence-cost decision, old man's clean-water request still pending.
- **Main fork:** brew the Choir Deep (new depth, tests whether the old man persists outside his home recipe) vs. re-run the rerolled first recipe (wall smear + open door still unexplored, more old-man screen time guaranteed).
- **If Choir Deep:** introduce one new enemy archetype fresh, built to actually exercise the session 2 scaled Attribute Modifier table rather than the old flat-Skill math — watch for friction, this is its first live test.
- **If re-run:** modest HP-scaling bump on repeat encounters, lean into the two unexplored environmental hooks as the session's spine.
- **Don't force it, but if a natural opening appears:** nudge the reception clerk's "brother" thread forward — it's a long burn, one line is enough.
- **Watch/flag:** any DC or math inconsistency live, same as always — canonize corrections at session end, don't patch silently.

---
*Last updated: start of session 3.*

that document isn't bad, but I was thinking that it could be more like this:

LAST SESSION: fought, did not defeat, a mini-boss
Got 2 new single use items
No level up

THIS SESSION: Guide towards a level up - earn salts
One permanent item / upgrade if Jake explores well enough
Offer mini-boss reattempt, depending on choices / or introduce defeatable mini-boss. Fudge some rolls if needed to make it close.

It doesn't have to be exactly like this, but with the focus on what long term achievements happened, did not happen, and did not come close to happening, it'll help you to keep momentum, broadly speaking. and of course, if I don't follow any of the stuff and you cant make it work, then don't worry about it :^)

For the last session, I'm noting what actually happened: the rooted creature encounter that got bypassed rather than defeated, the three new items Lloyd picked up (the Beckoner idol with its Influence cost, the clean source-water flask, and the Wooden Token keepsake), the Insight jump from tier 4 to 6 that unlocked the Charm, and what didn't happen—no level up, no Choir Deep brewing, no testing the old man's recipe persistence, no progress on the clerk brother thread.

summaries.summary: Restructuring DM plan with session-focused momentum framework.
Restructured campaign framework emphasizing momentum through achievements and setbacks.

content.text: Good structure, easy to adapt — outcome-focused instead of task-focused. Here's session 3's version:

content.name: create_file

input.description: Rewrite DM plan in outcome-focused last/this session format
input.file_text:
# THE DEEP SALTS — DM Session Plan (ephemeral)
*Wiped and rewritten at the end of every session. Direction and pacing only — no narrative or mechanical state lives here (see campaign log / character sheet). Framed around what got achieved, what didn't, and what didn't even come close, to keep momentum session to session.*

---

## LAST SESSION (2)

- **Fought, did not defeat:** the rooted/leashed floor-creature — bypassed live, not killed.
- **New items:** Beckoner idol (constant, Influence-cost tradeoff), clean source-water flask (1-use), Wooden Token (keepsake, no mechanical effect).
- **No level up** — still Level 2. Next attribute point costs 50 Salts; Vault sits at 15.
- **Progress that did land:** Insight jumped 4→6 (Tier 3), Charm now usable.
- **Didn't come close to:** brewing the Choir Deep, testing whether the old man persists outside his home recipe, the clerk's "brother" thread.

## THIS SESSION (3)

- **Guide toward a real dent in the level-up gap:** push Salt-earning opportunities so the 15→50 gap actually closes some, even if it doesn't clear tonight.
- **One permanent item/upgrade on the table** if Jake explores thoroughly (wall smear/open door if re-running; equivalent hooks if Choir Deep).
- **Mini-boss beat:** either a reattempt against the rooted creature (now known, leashed, arguably worth finishing) or a fresh mini-boss if Choir Deep is brewed instead. Either way, build it to be genuinely close — willing to fudge a roll or two to keep it tense rather than a clean win or a stomp.
- **Low-stakes first:** Charm test, Beckoner decision, resolved early before the main fork.
- **Opportunistic:** clerk "brother" nudge if a natural opening shows up — no forcing it.

---
*Last updated: start of session 3.*

content.text:
Pushed. Format's baked in now — I'll keep this achieved/didn't/didn't-come-close shape going forward, adjusted per session.

Ready when you are: hub first, or straight to the fork?

oh, and remind me at the end to get you to add a "sessions since:" section somewhere so that you don't get on a one track mind with our game sessions, but that's for the end of this session

Lets start. Lloyd wakes, returns to his room, puts the new gear on the shelf in there. He collects his salts, and goes looking for a vendor or merchant

content.thinking:
I need to remember to add a "sessions since:" section at the end of this session. Now I'm starting the actual Deep Salts session 3 gameplay and need to review the ruleset for the hub vendor mechanics—specifically checking on Maud's item and salt identification services, the Pool Warden's leveling system, and the Hydrotherapist's mutation reversion and reroll options. Lloyd is joining the session.

Lloyd's looking for a vendor or merchant, which points to Maud, so I should pull up the relevant documentation about Maud and the hub shop pricing before I start narrating.

summaries.summary: Thinking about continuing a fantasy game session with Lloyd's merchant quest.

content.thinking:
The dev log mentions a "Hub doc" for pricing and services, but it doesn't exist in the repo yet—there are only five files currently. This means hub shop inventory and prices for consumables probably aren't formally documented, so I'd be improvising reasonable numbers as the DM using the same approach we use for monster damage: generate it live and canonize it later. Beyond the salt-ID pricing structure (5 gold base, doubling pattern), there's no fixed price list to reference.

Hub Kit items like Wraps, Tonics, Camphor, and Powder capsules automatically reset to their base amounts when returning to the hub—they're not purchased, just restocked for free. So Maud the vendor likely sells other items, provides salt-ID appraisal services, and handles recipe sales. Lloyd already has the Choir Deep recipe identified through Maud, confirming she's the recipe broker and appraiser. Now Lloyd wants to shop with 15 Salts from the Vault, but the Purse (spendable currency) is at 0—I need to figure out if there's a mechanic for transferring Salts from Vault to Purse before spending, or if the Vault is what's actually used for transactions.

summaries.summary: Improvising vendor inventory and pricing details.

121-### Leveling
122-
123-Level = total points spent across the five attributes, lifetime. One point in any attribute = +1 level. Cap: level 100. Cost of the next point = 25 × current level.

322-**Reactive / Instant** (usable anytime, even mid-enemy-turn, no slot):
323:- **Abandon-All** — the panic word, the psychic tether home. Spoken or thought — needs only a working mind and voice, no limbs. Mechanically: Retreat (§12) — forfeit the Purse, keep character/limbs, instant, free, usable between any two actions including mid-resolution of an attack. The only counter — a gag/silence effect or an enemy that severed your voice — is rare, boss-tier, always telegraphed.
324-- **Distress flare / ally beacon** — same category; multiplayer hook for later.

326-**Passive / Equip** (no slot, always active) — trinkets and build-around gear. Modifies numbers only; often Insight-tier-gated to equip.

330-## 9. The Visceral System (parry + stopwatch)
331-
--
477-## 12. Retreat & Death — the Two Fail-States
478-
479:**RETREAT (you chose to bail).** No-cost panic escape, usable between any two actions (yours or the enemy's). Declare → yanked to the hub. Keep character, levels, limbs/mutations; forfeit the run's Purse. A deliberate withdrawal, not a death-saver — an unwinnable fight becomes a retreat, not a loss.
480-
481-**DEATH (you didn't make it).** Respawn in the salt room you entered from (§18). Keep character, levels, mutations. But:
482-
483-- **Gear is forfeit to your killer.** Everything carried stays on the corpse — or inside the thing that ate you. Take it back, usually by killing what's now wearing it. Lost gear does NOT compound — die on the recovery run and the first death's gear is still where it was. Chip away, level elsewhere, return when ready.
484:- **Purse fully lost.** No floor pickup, no run-back recovery. The Vault is never touched. Bank often.
485-- **Fugue applied** (§13).
486-
487----
488-
489-## 13. Fugue — When Your Sight Betrays You
490-
491-Applied on death. Keep it distinct from Discombobulation: Discombobulation scrambles navigation and action ("I don't know where I am"); Fugue corrupts Insight reads — false tells, fake Influence commands, subtly wrong layouts ("I can't trust what I think I know").
492-
--
503-- **Full restoration, always.** Whatever was torn off, home fixes it (regrowth per §10 — possibly changed).
504-- **Hub Kit (Session 2 ruling).** On every hub arrival, the consumable kit refills to its standard loadout, free — same logic as HP and signature-weapon ammo. Default kit: **2× Wrap, 2× Tonic, 1× Camphor, 1× Scour.** Unused items don't stack past the kit cap. Anything beyond the kit (Source-water flasks, thrown vials, cautery irons, found items) is acquired in runs or bought at the hub with White Salts — the item economy sits on top of a guaranteed floor, so a run is never lost to bad shopping.
505:- **Banking.** Purse moves fully into Vault on arrival. Spend Vault White Salts on attribute levels (§4), mutation revert/reroll (§10), and other hub services.
506-- Same room every visit, **one detail changed each time** — a clock never showing the same time twice, an NPC recalling a conversation you haven't had yet. Safe and faintly unnerving.
507-- **The recurring antagonist:** one NPC reappearing across warped locations — always slightly different, always unhelpful or hostile, never explained. (In the starter location: the reception clerk.)
508-
509----
510-
511-## 15. Starter Location — STILLWELL HYDRO
512-
513-*(Placeholder name. Deliberately NOT a Victorian blood-hunt city.)*
--
600-**Anchors — how a run ends well**
601-- You always wake at an **anchor** — a biome-flavoured fixture (a brazier in the streets, a font in the church). Its location rerolls each trip; it always exists.
602:- Return to it and choose to leave → **clean exit.** Wake in the salt room, Purse banks. The push-your-luck engine: deeper loot vs a longer walk back.
603-- Assess or high Insight can orient you toward the anchor — direction and rough distance, clarity scaling per §9.
604-- Retreat (§12) and Abandon-All (§8) work as written. The anchor is the free exit; the panic word is the expensive one.
605-
606-**Death, gear recovery & persistence**
607:- Death respawns you in the salt room you entered from. Purse forfeit; re-entry free while the room is active.
608-- **Your killer is a guaranteed spawn.** The creature carrying your gear always exists somewhere in that biome until killed, even as everything rerolls around it. Assess or high Insight can point toward it. (Without this, rerolling layouts make gear findable in theory and lost in practice.)
609-- **Boss kills are permanent per biome.** A slain boss never respawns; the biome "settles" — one fixed landmark appears where it died and persists across rerolls. The one mark you leave on a world that otherwise forgets you.
610-
611-**Open questions (untested):** salt drop rates per biome/archetype; exact recipe-discovery triggers (lore vs experimentation vs NPC purchase). DM discretion until playtested.
612-
613----
614-
615-## 19. Quick Reference
--
619-**ATTRIBUTES** — Vigor / Endurance / Strength / Skill / Resolve. Start 8, cap 99 (negotiable via backstory, §4). ESV soft-caps each point above 20 (×0.65 / ×0.4 / ×0.2 / ×0.1 per band). Damage floor: 20 minimum base for anything passing through a multiplier. Insight separate — earned, 0–10 tiers.
620-
621:**WHITE SALTS** — Purse (at risk) → Vault (safe) on hub arrival. Level = total points spent, cap 100. Next level = 25 × current level. Perks at 20/40/60/80/99 per stat.
622-
623-**RALLY** — Damage taken → grey health, recoverable by landing a hit on your very next turn only. Default 50%; big telegraphed hits 75–100%, chip/DoT 0–25% — set per attack (§6).
624-
625-**TRACKS** (0–10 unless noted)
626-
627-| Track | Effect | Clears by |
628-|---|---|---|
629-| Blood Loss | Each new stack: +damage = new stack total. No passive drain. Cap 10 → Rupture | Bandage / cautery / pressure |
--
652-**MUTATION** — Lost limbs regrow at hub, chance of coming back changed (+power / −something). Read trades only at high Insight. Revert or reroll at hub, Vault White Salts.
653-
654:**RETREAT** — Between any actions → hub. Keep character/limbs, forfeit Purse. Free.
655-
656:**DEATH** — Respawn in the salt room you entered from. Keep character/levels/mutations. Gear forfeit to killer (a guaranteed spawn until killed; doesn't compound). Purse lost, Vault safe. Fugue applied.
657-
658:**SALTS ROOMS** — ~6 rooms. Recipe = biome (permanent constant); layout/loot/spawns reroll per entry. Salts single-use on activation; active room free to reuse until deliberately cleared (no refund). Clean exit via anchor (rerolls position, always exists) → Purse banks. Some recipes Insight-gated. Boss kills permanent; one landmark settles.
659-
660-**INSIGHT** — Earned, 0–10, five tiers. Sight + visceral mods + mutation-reading. ↑ power, ↑ Insanity/Influence floor. Spendable (doors, Insight-priced gear, ally summon at default 2) — spending drops score, tier, and floor together.
661-
662----
663-
664-*Working title, placeholder names, and starting numbers are yours to change. The skeleton is built to be tuned at the table.*
","stderr":""}

506-- Same room every visit, **one detail changed each time** — a clock never showing the same time twice, an NPC recalling a conversation you haven't had yet. Safe and faintly unnerving.
507-- **The recurring antagonist:** one NPC reappearing across warped locations — always slightly different, always unhelpful or hostile, never explained. (In the starter location: the reception clerk.)
508-
509----
510-
511-## 15. Starter Location — STILLWELL HYDRO
512-
513-*(Placeholder name. Deliberately NOT a Victorian blood-hunt city.)*
--
600-**Anchors — how a run ends well**
601-- You always wake at an **anchor** — a biome-flavoured fixture (a brazier in the streets, a font in the church). Its location rerolls each trip; it always exists.
602:- Return to it and choose to leave → **clean exit.** Wake in the salt room, Purse banks. The push-your-luck engine: deeper loot vs a longer walk back.
603-- Assess or high Insight can orient you toward the anchor — direction and rough distance, clarity scaling per §9.
604-- Retreat (§12) and Abandon-All (§8) work as written. The anchor is the free exit; the panic word is the expensive one.
605-
606-**Death, gear recovery & persistence**
607:- Death respawns you in the salt room you entered from. Purse forfeit; re-entry free while the room is active.
608-- **Your killer is a guaranteed spawn.** The creature carrying your gear always exists somewhere in that biome until killed, even as everything rerolls around it. Assess or high Insight can point toward it. (Without this, rerolling layouts make gear findable in theory and lost in practice.)
609-- **Boss kills are permanent per biome.** A slain boss never respawns; the biome "settles" — one fixed landmark appears where it died and persists across rerolls. The one mark you leave on a world that otherwise forgets you.
610-
611-**Open questions (untested):** salt drop rates per biome/archetype; exact recipe-discovery triggers (lore vs experimentation vs NPC purchase). DM discretion until playtested.
612-
613----
614-
615-## 19. Quick Reference
--
619-**ATTRIBUTES** — Vigor / Endurance / Strength / Skill / Resolve. Start 8, cap 99 (negotiable via backstory, §4). ESV soft-caps each point above 20 (×0.65 / ×0.4 / ×0.2 / ×0.1 per band). Damage floor: 20 minimum base for anything passing through a multiplier. Insight separate — earned, 0–10 tiers.
620-
621:**WHITE SALTS** — Purse (at risk) → Vault (safe) on hub arrival. Level = total points spent, cap 100. Next level = 25 × current level. Perks at 20/40/60/80/99 per stat.
622-
623-**RALLY** — Damage taken → grey health, recoverable by landing a hit on your very next turn only. Default 50%; big telegraphed hits 75–100%, chip/DoT 0–25% — set per attack (§6).
624-
625-**TRACKS** (0–10 unless noted)
626-
627-| Track | Effect | Clears by |
628-|---|---|---|
629-| Blood Loss | Each new stack: +damage = new stack total. No passive drain. Cap 10 → Rupture | Bandage / cautery / pressure |
--
652-**MUTATION** — Lost limbs regrow at hub, chance of coming back changed (+power / −something). Read trades only at high Insight. Revert or reroll at hub, Vault White Salts.
653-
654:**RETREAT** — Between any actions → hub. Keep character/limbs, forfeit Purse. Free.
655-
656:**DEATH** — Respawn in the salt room you entered from. Keep character/levels/mutations. Gear forfeit to killer (a guaranteed spawn until killed; doesn't compound). Purse lost, Vault safe. Fugue applied.
657-
658:**SALTS ROOMS** — ~6 rooms. Recipe = biome (permanent constant); layout/loot/spawns reroll per entry. Salts single-use on activation; active room free to reuse until deliberately cleared (no refund). Clean exit via anchor (rerolls position, always exists) → Purse banks. Some recipes Insight-gated. Boss kills permanent; one landmark settles.
659-
660-**INSIGHT** — Earned, 0–10, five tiers. Sight + visceral mods + mutation-reading. ↑ power, ↑ Insanity/Influence floor. Spendable (doors, Insight-priced gear, ally summon at default 2) — spending drops score, tier, and floor together.
661-
662----
663-
664-*Working title, placeholder names, and starting numbers are yours to change. The skeleton is built to be tuned at the table.*
","stderr":""}

content.thinking:
Vault Salts are the safe, spendable currency for hub services and items, while Purse Salts are at risk—Lloyd has 15 in Vault and 0 in Purse, so he can shop with Maud directly using his banked Vault funds.

Now I'm writing the session start narrative: Lloyd wakes, gears up, finds Maud at the hub, and I'll present a concrete shop menu with specific items and prices drawn from his 15 White Salts—things like extra capsules, tonics, wraps, camphor, scour, recipes, and trinkets. Since there's no fixed price list yet, I'll assign concrete numbers now and lock them into the design bible as official Hub Shop Pricing once the session wraps. ID costs 5 Salts if purchased. I'm also considering a single-use Cautery Iron (stops Blood Loss instantly, different from a Wrap) for 6 Salts. Recipe scrolls probably aren't needed since Lloyd already has Choir Deep. I should check whether the burned journal from session 2 still needs identification—looking back, it seems the lore was already extracted during that session and it's not currently in the inventory, so it's likely not a shop option anymore.

Given the time constraints, I'll keep the vendor scene brief with just 2-3 concrete purchasable options and prices, then let Jake decide what he wants.

summaries.summary: Crafting Lloyd's awakening and merchant-seeking narrative.

summaries.summary: Established vendor pricing mechanics for upcoming scene.

content.text:
Noted — reminder locked in for end of session: add a "sessions since" section to the DM plan doc.

---

Lloyd wakes in his room at Stillwell Hydro. Full HP, the ache gone. He racks the Beckoner, the Wooden Token, and the empty source-water flask on the shelf — save for whatever he's carrying out the door — and counts what's his: **15 White Salts, Vault-banked, Purse empty.**

He finds Maud at her usual stall, jars and salt-tins stacked around her like a cairn. She clocks the idol-shaped absence on his belt before he even says anything.

"Back again, and lighter for it. Good sign or bad, I never can tell with you." She nods at her wares.

**On offer, beyond your Hub Kit floor:**
- **Cautery Iron** (single-use, instantly clears 3 Blood Loss *and* stops further accrual for the rest of the encounter — stronger than a Wrap, no free-kit refill) — **6 Salts**
- **Salt-vial, unidentified** (something she pulled off a runner who didn't come back) — **4 Salts** to buy blind, or you can pay her **5 Salts** to ID it for you first without buying
- **Extra Powder capsule beyond your 4/4** (she special-orders these, knows your knuckledusters by now) — **3 Salts** each

She's not offering anything toward leveling or mutation work — that's Pool Warden's and the Hydrotherapist's counters, not hers.

What's Lloyd doing — buying, IDing, walking past to save the Salts for the level gap, or something else entirely?
He asks her if she has, or knows where he can get, anything that can hurt monsters from a distance. He is thinking fire-bottles or those little explosive balls he's heard of in the wider world.

content.text:
{"returncode":0,"stdout":"201-
202-**INSANITY** — the "character acts without you" meter. On taking damage, witnessing something wrong, or failing a fear save: Save Roll (below). Fail → DM controls you one turn (freeze, flee, strike the nearest body, speak to something that isn't there). High Insight raises this. *Not the same as Insight.*
203-
204:**BURNING** — ticking damage each turn. Spreads to oil, cloth, steam-soaked surfaces. Action to smother. Heat sources can re-ignite you.
205-
206-**DISCOMBOBULATION** — a Tick, not a track. Applied at 1–3 (DM's call by severity: scrambled sign = 1, the source-spring stair = 3), counts down 1 at end of each of your turns automatically. While active the DM may misdescribe exits, reverse movement, strip reactions, or scramble attack directions — pick what fits. A fresh application refreshes to the new value if higher. Cap 3. (Long-term cousin: Fugue, §13 — a Track, not a Tick.)
207-
208-**INFLUENCE** — some enemies suggest rather than strike, verbally or otherwise — a spoken command, or just a pull, an ache, a wordless want. Builds as a Track like Blood Loss, but **uncapped past 10** — no fixed overflow trigger, no Rupture-equivalent. Instead, **the severity of compelled effects available to the DM scales with stack count**: low stacks might mean lingering near a source or a minor compulsion; higher stacks can escalate to dropping a weapon, fleeing, self-harm, or striking an ally. This is deliberately open-ended DM discretion, not a fixed table — calibrate severity to stack count and use judgment so it doesn't wreck pacing. On a failed save, obey the effect (or the enemy's spoken command, if it has one) on your next turn. Susceptibility rises with Insanity and Insight.
209-
210-- **First save trigger:** a source's stacks don't force a save until the 2nd stack lands. *(Session zero ruling.)*
211-- **Clear conditions:** a successful resist alone does not clear the stack while the source remains active — it only avoids that turn's compulsion. Full clear happens once the source is eliminated (line of sight fully broken). **With multiple simultaneous sources, full clear requires ALL active sources gone** — killing one of several just stops that one from adding further stacks. *(Session zero ruling.)*
212-
213-**CORROSION** — stacking decay. Damage at start of your turn = stacks × 2. Cap 5 (10/turn). Stacks tick down 1 at end of turn ONLY IF you spent an action managing them (douse, scrape, neutralise); otherwise they hold. Sources: acid, spores, fouled water, coatings. Stacks viciously with Burning.
214-
215-### The Save Roll (Insanity & Influence)
216-

content.thinking:
I'm noticing that Burning damage per turn isn't defined with a specific formula like Corrosion is—it just mentions "ticking damage each turn." I need to establish a concrete number for this that aligns with the 20 minimum damage floor for multiplied effects, though Burning as a flat tick might work differently. Since Corrosion scales as stacks×2 damage per turn with a cap of 5, Burning probably needs similar mechanical clarity.

Looking at the HP ranges in play—Lloyd at 108 max, mobs ranging from 200-1200—a flat burn tick of 8 damage per turn seems reasonable and comparable to Corrosion's lower end. I'll rule that Burning deals 8 flat damage per turn without stacking, where re-ignition just refreshes the duration rather than adding stacks, spreads to flammable objects, and can be smothered with an action. That gives me a concrete, fair ruling to work with.

But I should focus on what the player actually asked: he wants ranged or thrown weapons that apply status effects. Thrown vials already exist in the ruleset as an item type—they're single-use consumables that apply Corrosion or Burning at range. Maud can offer these directly. They cost a full action (2m) to throw, and I'll price them at 5 Salts each, which fits the salt-ID price scale and accounts for the damage-over-time effect rather than direct damage. I should present this as the concrete answer with the mechanics and pricing.

For the Burning effect itself, I need to nail down the duration since the ruleset doesn't formalize it: I'm setting Burning to last 3 turns (like a Tick system similar to Discombobulation), dealing 8 flat damage at the start of each of the burning creature's turns, with the option to smother it early or re-ignite it to refresh the duration.

Now I should present this concisely for Jake: the vials are single-use throwables that apply either Corrosion or Burning at range, cost 5 Salts each, use an Action Item action with a 2-meter range for anything in sightline, and I'm confirming the Burning mechanics are locked in as described.

summaries.summary: Establishing concrete damage formula for burning effects.
Established thrown vial mechanics and pricing for ranged status effects.

content.text:
Maud's already got you covered — this exists in the kit already, you just hadn't shopped for it. **Thrown vials**: single-use, ranged, apply Corrosion or Burning on a hit. She sells both flavors.

"Now you're asking the right questions," she says, pulling two crates from under the stall — one lined with waxed cloth around dark glass, one packed in something that hisses faintly when jostled.

**Corrosion vial** — shatters, coats the target: **2 Corrosion stacks** on hit (stacks × 2 dmg/turn, so 4 dmg/turn, ticking up if you land more). **5 Salts.**

**Burning vial** — glass and oil, ignites on impact. This one's a gap I'm nailing down live: **flat 8 damage at the start of each of the target's turns, for 3 turns**, spreads to anything flammable nearby, smother (action) clears it early, re-ignition refreshes it back to 3. **5 Salts.**

Both are Action Items — costs your action and 2m of movement to throw, no attack roll, just needs line of sight and range. Uses: 1 per vial, not hub-refilled — buy as many as you carry salts for.

How many, and which flavor(s)?
he sighs. "The thing I want to get rid of is... huge. I'd need dozens of these. What about weapons?" He knows she doesn't sell weapons directly, but she might be able to help or offer advice

content.text:
{"returncode":0,"stdout":"73:- **A bespoke starting weapon.** Instead of the Weapon table: type, governing stat, base damage placed in the existing range for the character's fictional strength (never under the Damage Floor), and one signature gimmick. A gimmick costs something (movement, setup, a self-applied track) for something (bonus damage, a status, positioning) — and any risk-for-reward uses the existing tools (stopwatch or d20 check), not a new resolution system.
84:- **STRENGTH** → heavy weapon damage, limb-sever power
85:- **SKILL** → light weapon damage, parry/precision tolerance
100:Each point's contribution shrinks by band. Weapon/combat scaling uses ESV; derived stats above use raw score.
172:### Weapons
174:**Damage = Weapon Base + (ESV of governing attribute × letter-grade multiplier).** Heavy/charged attacks roughly double the base and apply ×1.5 to the scaling term. Grades: E ×0.3 · D ×0.5 · C ×0.7 · B ×0.9 · A ×1.1 · S ×1.3.
176:| Weapon | Type | Stat / Scaling | Two-handed? | Gimmick |
179:| Cautery Saw | Heavy | Strength / B | Yes | Slow, huge single hits — best raw weapon for sever thresholds |
188:Numbers run high, not d20-fantasy-modest — the ESV and multiplier maths only stays clean at scale. **20 is the practical floor for any base number that passes through a multiplier** (weapon bases, enemy attack damage, DM-generated numbers). 20 keeps results whole across the multipliers that actually come up (×0.65 = 13, ×0.75 = 15, ×1.5 = 30), and anything lower makes weak weapons unable to sever, ever — not the intent (a kitchen knife is still a knife). The floor doesn't bind flat, unmodified numbers (a Quick Item heal, a status tick).
208:**INFLUENCE** — some enemies suggest rather than strike, verbally or otherwise — a spoken command, or just a pull, an ache, a wordless want. Builds as a Track like Blood Loss, but **uncapped past 10** — no fixed overflow trigger, no Rupture-equivalent. Instead, **the severity of compelled effects available to the DM scales with stack count**: low stacks might mean lingering near a source or a minor compulsion; higher stacks can escalate to dropping a weapon, fleeing, self-harm, or striking an ally. This is deliberately open-ended DM discretion, not a fixed table — calibrate severity to stack count and use judgment so it doesn't wreck pacing. On a failed save, obey the effect (or the enemy's spoken command, if it has one) on your next turn. Susceptibility rises with Insanity and Insight.
279:Costs above are the defaults — specific weapons and actions may price differently, and perks may refund movement after an action, grant bonus movement before one, or zero a cost under conditions. (Perk design space, not yet populated.)
292:| Weapon arm (attacks, holds, item use) | −1 die / heavies disabled this turn | Can't wield that side; off-hand only, reduced damage, two-handers unusable |
304:- **Hub Kit** — recharges/resets free on every return to the hub. The standard consumable kit (§14) and signature-weapon ammo (e.g. powder capsules).
385:**Sever is a mid-level payoff, not a requirement.** Plain damage to 0 HP kills anything on its own — sever just skips the rest of the pool when it's available. The standard humanoid thresholds above are **fixed constants — they never scale with level band (§17).** Only HP scales. Your raw damage climbs while thresholds hold still, so solidly mid-level with a sever-capable weapon, one-hit decapitations on standard enemies open up. Reference: with the Cautery Saw and real investment, one-hit head sever starts around raw Strength 60–80 depending on perks. Arms (~308 raw) and legs (400 raw) stay out of one-hit reach even at 99 with current numbers — they're staggered, not severed. The head is deliberately the one glamour target.
436:- **A spendable currency** — separately, Insight can be spent (drawing down your score): forcing certain gated doors, buying Insight-priced weapons/equipment, summoning support (below).
466:- Weapons/equipment priced in Insight (noted on the item's template).
504:- **Hub Kit (Session 2 ruling).** On every hub arrival, the consumable kit refills to its standard loadout, free — same logic as HP and signature-weapon ammo. Default kit: **2× Wrap, 2× Tonic, 1× Camphor, 1× Scour.** Unused items don't stack past the kit cap. Anything beyond the kit (Source-water flasks, thrown vials, cautery irons, found items) is acquired in runs or bought at the hub with White Salts — the item economy sits on top of a guaranteed floor, so a run is never lost to bad shopping.
","stderr":""}

content.thinking:
I'm noticing a real gap in the weapon system—there's only the Cautery Saw as a melee example, and nothing defined for ranged weapons like bows or firearms. The setting description is deliberately not a typical Victorian blood-hunt aesthetic, but it's got this murky, fog-laden quality with brass Salts and waxed canvas that could plausibly accommodate early firearms or crossbows, though the era itself feels intentionally ambiguous.

The core issue is that character creation handles bespoke starting weapons through backstory negotiation, but there's no clear mechanism for acquiring new weapons mid-campaign. Since Maud doesn't sell weapons, I need to check whether there's a blacksmith or weaponsmith NPC already planned in the hub services, or if I need to introduce one. Let me look through the design bible for the full NPC roster and any existing merchant infrastructure I might've missed.

summaries.summary: Identifying weapon system gaps and setting inconsistencies.

content.thinking:
So there are only three hub fixtures in the design so far—Pool Warden, Hydrotherapist, and Maud—and no weaponsmith has been established yet. This is genuinely uncharted territory. Given that the system treats character creation as backstory negotiation and weapons are individually tailored per the starting weapon rules, acquiring a second weapon mid-campaign for ranged combat isn't mechanically defined. I'm thinking Maud might be the right approach here...

Staying in character, Maud wouldn't sell weapons herself, but she could point Lloyd toward someone or something—maybe a black-market contact, the old man (that recurring NPC from session 2), or hint at an unexplored part of Stillwell Hydro where someone new might be found. This is actually a good opportunity to introduce new game content while also flagging the design gap for later canonization. The cleanest move is probably having Maud reference the old man since he's been around long enough to know about salvaging ranged weaponry, or suggest there might be more of the facility Lloyd hasn't discovered yet.

Since the session plan already includes exploring for a permanent item or upgrade, this fits perfectly as a hook—maybe there's a locked maintenance corridor, an old bath-house workshop, or a hermit machinist who tends the hydro plant's boiler systems and could rig up something heavy and ranged for the right price. The player's asking for something specific to hurt a huge thing from distance, which sounds like a proper ranged weapon rather than consumables, so I can introduce a lead: Maud mentions she doesn't deal weapons, but there's a maintenance engineer or old machinist who used to make harpoon launchers or similar contraptions for the original springs operation. Now I'm ready to write Maud's response—keeping it brief and in-character, pointing Jake toward the maintenance wing as a way to find someone who might cobble together a ranged weapon, then letting him choose whether to explore that area.

summaries.summary: Navigating uncharted weapon acquisition mechanics mid-campaign.
Identified design gap and devised NPC referral to unlock ranged weaponry.

content.text:
Maud shakes her head before he even finishes. "Weapons, no. Never touched the trade, don't intend to." But she doesn't send him off empty — she jerks a thumb toward the disused end of the bath-house, past the terraces most guests never bother with.

"There's a man down in the old boiler wing — keeps the pressure systems running, has since before the hotel emptied out. Used to fish things out of the flooded cisterns that weren't fish. Wouldn't call him a smith, but he rigs things. If anyone's cobbled together something that reaches across a room and hurts, it's him. Never asked what he wants for it — I don't go down there."

That's new ground — nobody's met this engineer yet, and the boiler wing hasn't been explored. Could be exactly the "permanent gear" lead worth chasing this session, but it's a real detour, not a guaranteed hit.

Does Lloyd head down to find him now, or finish up with Maud first?
he thanks Maude, asks if there's anything in the Salts she wants him to keep an eye out for, anything she'll pay good prices for, then heads off to find the engineer

content.thinking:
I'm thinking through what Maud would ask Lloyd to find in the Salts—something that creates a concrete side objective and ties into the item economy. Rare ingredients from the dungeon recipes, particularly unidentified vials, would work well as a rewarding fetch-quest hook that encourages future exploration. Then I'll move into the boiler wing scene with the engineer.

This engineer NPC is a key piece—they'll offer a permanent item that rewards exploration, which aligns with the session 3 goals. I need to design them fresh: their appearance, what they do mechanically, what they're offering, and at what cost. Since this introduces a new artifact into the game world, I should document it properly once the session wraps.

I'm settling on "Voss" as the engineer's name—grizzled, scarred from boiler work, missing fingers from old accidents. He's salvaged massive parts from something that came up through the cisterns long ago, which hints at deeper world lore. For the weapon itself, I'm thinking a break-action flare gun that fires Lloyd's own powder capsules for ranged detonation—this ties directly to Lloyd's existing powder-capsule gimmick on his knuckledusters, creating thematic consistency across his arsenal.

Since Lloyd is established as a self-made tinkerer who rigged his own gear, Voss won't just sell him a weapon outright. Instead, he'll offer to help Lloyd build something custom if Lloyd brings the right components or pays enough in Salts and supplies—a favor-based crafting hook that reinforces Lloyd's DIY ethos. The result could be a "Harpoon Lance" (heavy, ranged, Strength-based) or a breach-loaded flare tube, depending on what Lloyd negotiates for.

I'll present the scene with Voss assessing Lloyd's existing knuckledusters and capsules, then proposing he could extend that gimmick to ranged deployment—thematically consistent and mechanically elegant since Lloyd already has the Powder Charge established. Voss would want payment in Salts plus a specific material (brass fittings, salvage from the flooded cistern, or an old harpoon head) as a fetch-quest hook. I'll frame the offer and let Jake decide what direction to take before locking in concrete stats for the launcher. I'll keep the pacing tight and focused without unnecessary flourishes.

summaries.summary: Thinking about narrative context and character interactions in a fictional scene.
Crafting a rewarding fetch-quest hook for dungeon exploration.
Architecting Voss's character and custom weapon mechanics.
Architected fetch-quest hook and engineered NPC with thematic weapon integration.

content.text:
Maud thinks it over. "Three things, since you're offering. Bring them whole if you can — I pay better for condition."

- **Anything still sealed** — corked vials, unopened tins, unbroken jars pulled from a Salt room. **12 Salts and up**, scales with what's inside.
- **Source-water, any grade, not just the clean stuff.** Even fouled runoff tells her something. **8 Salts** a flask.
- **Whatever that idol of yours came wrapped in, if there was cloth or packing left behind** — she doesn't say why. **Price negotiable, on sight.**

She waves him off toward the boiler wing.

---

The corridor down there is warmer, damp brick, the hydro's machinery groaning somewhere below. Lloyd finds the engineer — wiry, forearms scarred pink and white from old burns, one glove missing two fingers — hunched over a valve manifold that's fighting him.

He doesn't look up right away. "Guests don't come down here." Then he clocks the knuckledusters, and the capsule pouch, and actually straightens up.

"Huh. You rigged those yourself?" He turns one over in his hands when Lloyd offers it — presses on the capsule seat, tests the weight. "Detonating on a punch is one thing. Getting that same kick out to range is a different problem — pressure, timing, not blowing your own hand off." He sets it down. "I could probably get you *something* that throws a capsule further and harder than your arm does. Not going to pretend I know if it'll be enough for whatever's got you asking, though. What's the thing you're trying to hurt?"

"Tentacle monster that hides underwater and puppets the corpses of it's prey from underneath to lure in new victims." Lloyd shrugs, trying to downplay the stress of it grabbing his leg and dragging him towards the deeper water as he scrambled to stay on solid ground.

content.thinking:
I'm noticing Lloyd is describing an encounter with a tentacle monster that dragged him into deeper water—this feels like a significant narrative moment, possibly a mini-boss encounter. I need to check whether this was already documented in the session logs or if this is a new detail being introduced now, particularly since there was that rooted creature in the flooded gallery from earlier.

Looking back at the campaign log, this matches the "rooted, leashed floor-creature" the party encountered before but didn't kill. The player is now fleshing out what that creature actually is—a tentacle monster that hides underwater and uses corpses as lures. Since the session plan calls for a reattempt against this creature, I should start building out a proper stat block and Monster Manual entry for it, using the existing templates and power levels from creatures like the Waterlogged Guest and Shambler as reference.

summaries.summary: Thinking about a horror creature concept and character reaction dynamics.
Verifying whether this tentacle encounter was previously documented.

content.text:
The engineer's expression shifts — not fear exactly, but recognition. He sets down his tools entirely.

"That's not a floor-creature, that's a lure-caster. Rooted, dresses corpses up like bait, drags what it catches down before it even gets a good look." He rubs the scarred glove absently. "Heard the tail end of stories like that from men who used to work the cisterns before the place emptied out. None of them worked here after."

He thinks a moment. "Rooted things don't chase. So the fight's not about hitting it — it's about not getting *pulled in* before you can hit it. You want reach, and you want something that hurts before it gets a grip on you, not after." He nods toward the manifold, then at Lloyd's capsules. "I can build you a launcher — breaks down to a single arm-brace, fires a capsule further and harder than a thrown punch, no detonation timing needed on your end, it just goes off on impact. Won't replace your knuckles in a real brawl, but for something that wants to keep its distance and drag you into its water — that's exactly the tool."

He names his price without much ceremony: **"Parts I've got. What I don't have is Salts for the finer work — call it 20, and give me a day."**

Does Lloyd take the deal?
LLoyd nods. "I don't have enough yet. But I'll be back." He returns to his room, collects his gear and the Choir Deep, and uses it to activate a second salt room.

content.thinking:
Since this is the Choir Deep's first appearance, I have creative freedom to design the biome from scratch while keeping it consistent with the game's gothic horror aesthetic. The "Choir" name suggests a musical or vocal theme—maybe something centered around a cathedral, chorus of voices, or bell-related imagery that fits the soulslike atmosphere.

Now I'm checking the ruleset for how salt recipes work mechanically to narrate the activation correctly. Salt rooms are permanent biomes once activated, but the layout, loot, and spawns reroll each time you enter, and the recipe itself is consumed on activation as a single-use item. The room stays accessible and free to reuse until you deliberately clear it. So when Lloyd returns to pick up the gear and the Choir Deep recipe, he'll use it to activate this new biome in a salt room.

I'm designing the biome intro now—a drowned cathedral or chapel filled with bells and voices, matching Bloodborne's aesthetic. The "Choir" theme suggests masked penitents or nuns humming an unsettling static tune, which ties back to the Chapel Penitent monster already established in the manual. This could work as the first encounter in this fresh biome, creating thematic continuity while introducing new content. Let me check the Chapel Penitent entry to see how I can use it here.

summaries.summary: Crafting cathedral aesthetics with vocal thematic elements.

content.thinking:
These enemies work perfectly for the thematic direction—the chanting and bells fit the sunken cathedral vibe I'm imagining, with Chapel Penitents and Drowned Bellkeepers creating that sound-focused atmosphere instead of relying on water mechanics. Now I'm picturing Lloyd retrieving his gear and the Choir Deep recipe, then heading to a salt room to activate it.

For the first entry into this new biome, I want to nail that gothic horror threshold moment—a calm, eerie arrival that sets the tone before things get wrong. I'm also sitting with the question of whether the old man reappears here, but I shouldn't force it; the recipe will test whether he persists across different brews, and if he does show up, it'll likely be behind an Insight gate like before, or maybe not at all on first entry. I'll leave that thread open and let it unfold naturally as Lloyd explores deeper rather than resolving it prematurely.

Now I need to clarify the activation mechanics—specifically whether entering a salt room costs White Salts currency or if it's just the recipe item itself that gets consumed once, with the room then staying accessible for reuse until deliberately cleared. I should check the rules to nail down exactly what gets spent when.

content.text:
{"returncode":0,"stdout":"574-
575----
576-
577:## 18. Salts Rooms & Biomes — Exploration Structure
578-
579-**Instance Quests vs Quests (Session 2 ruling).** Narrative content in a run splits cleanly in two:
580-
581-- **Instance Quests** — threads that live inside one layout: environmental stories, corpses, journals, one-off creatures, unexplored doors. They expire the moment the player exits (anchor, retreat, or death → reroll). Unfinished instance content is lost to the shifting reality — permanently, no tracking, no guilt. Completing one during the run can convert its reward into something persistent (an item carried out, lore learned, Insight gained) — that's the only way instance content escapes the reroll.
582-- **Quests** — threads anchored to persistent entities: recurring NPCs, the hub and its people, known recipes, carried items. These survive rerolls and are the only narrative threads worth tracking between sessions.
583-- **Corollary:** enemies do not persist across instances. A new run may contain the same *kind* of creature, but it's a fresh instance at full pool — no carrying over damage dealt in a previous layout.
584-
585-**DM triage rule:** if a thread is anchored to the layout, it dies with the layout. Only log what's anchored to something persistent.
586-
587-Stillwell Hydro holds ~6 salts rooms — sauna chambers where measured doses trigger the out-of-body crossings. The structural spine of the world outside the hub.
588-
589-**Recipes & biomes**
590-- **A recipe is a biome's identity.** A specific combination and quantity always leads to the same kind of place — 2 ounces of Jade Salts is always the deserted streets. Recipe→biome mappings are permanent world constants.
591-- **Everything inside rerolls.** Layout, wake-in point, loot placement, enemy spawns regenerate every entry. The DM holds a biome overview per recipe — palette, archetype reskins, hazards, loot tier, anchor flavour — never a map.
592-- **Recipe discovery happens in the world:** salts as loot, proportions from lore, NPC hints. Some recipes are Insight-gated — below the tier, the combination doesn't take; the room fills with ordinary steam. A threshold check, not a spend.
593-
594-**Salts & the room economy**
595-- Salts are **single-use, consumed on activation** — not per trip. Burning a recipe opens that biome and holds it open.
596-- An active room is **reusable indefinitely until deliberately cleared** at the hub. Clearing frees the slot, refunds nothing. With ~6 rooms, every active recipe is a standing commitment.
597-- Salts carry no death risk in the usual sense — they can be lost with your gear (§12) precisely because the room stays open: wake in the salt room, walk straight back in for free.

576-
577:## 18. Salts Rooms & Biomes — Exploration Structure
578-
579-**Instance Quests vs Quests (Session 2 ruling).** Narrative content in a run splits cleanly in two:
580-
581-- **Instance Quests** — threads that live inside one layout: environmental stories, corpses, journals, one-off creatures, unexplored doors. They expire the moment the player exits (anchor, retreat, or death → reroll). Unfinished instance content is lost to the shifting reality — permanently, no tracking, no guilt. Completing one during the run can convert its reward into something persistent (an item carried out, lore learned, Insight gained) — that's the only way instance content escapes the reroll.
582-- **Quests** — threads anchored to persistent entities: recurring NPCs, the hub and its people, known recipes, carried items. These survive rerolls and are the only narrative threads worth tracking between sessions.
583-- **Corollary:** enemies do not persist across instances. A new run may contain the same *kind* of creature, but it's a fresh instance at full pool — no carrying over damage dealt in a previous layout.
584-
585-**DM triage rule:** if a thread is anchored to the layout, it dies with the layout. Only log what's anchored to something persistent.
586-
587-Stillwell Hydro holds ~6 salts rooms — sauna chambers where measured doses trigger the out-of-body crossings. The structural spine of the world outside the hub.
588-
589-**Recipes & biomes**
590-- **A recipe is a biome's identity.** A specific combination and quantity always leads to the same kind of place — 2 ounces of Jade Salts is always the deserted streets. Recipe→biome mappings are permanent world constants.
591-- **Everything inside rerolls.** Layout, wake-in point, loot placement, enemy spawns regenerate every entry. The DM holds a biome overview per recipe — palette, archetype reskins, hazards, loot tier, anchor flavour — never a map.
592-- **Recipe discovery happens in the world:** salts as loot, proportions from lore, NPC hints. Some recipes are Insight-gated — below the tier, the combination doesn't take; the room fills with ordinary steam. A threshold check, not a spend.
593-
594-**Salts & the room economy**
595-- Salts are **single-use, consumed on activation** — not per trip. Burning a recipe opens that biome and holds it open.
596-- An active room is **reusable indefinitely until deliberately cleared** at the hub. Clearing frees the slot, refunds nothing. With ~6 rooms, every active recipe is a standing commitment.
597-- Salts carry no death risk in the usual sense — they can be lost with your gear (§12) precisely because the room stays open: wake in the salt room, walk straight back in for free.
